name: YHWH
shortDescription: YHWH is the principal name for God used in the Hebrew Bible, traditionally vocalized as Yahweh or Jehovah. He is considered the God of Israel and the ultimate authority in Judaism.
description: YHWH, often referred to as the Tetragrammaton, is the most sacred name of God in Judaism. Its origin is debated, but it's generally understood to derive from the Hebrew verb 'to be,' implying existence, becoming, or causing to be. YHWH is not merely a name, but a revelation of God's essential nature: eternal, self-sufficient, and actively involved in the world. He is the creator of the universe, the lawgiver, and the covenant maker with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. His role encompasses justice, mercy, and redemption. While depictions of YHWH are forbidden, He is described through His actions and interactions with humanity, particularly through prophets like Isaiah, Jeremiah, and others. The name YHWH is considered so holy that observant Jews traditionally avoid pronouncing it, substituting it with Adonai (Lord) or HaShem (The Name). The concept of YHWH evolves throughout the Hebrew Bible, from a tribal deity to a universal God, emphasizing monotheism and ethical behavior. The relationship between YHWH and humanity is central to Jewish theology, characterized by both divine command and divine love.
epithets: The Holy One, Blessed be He

sources.title: Ancient Israel: From Abraham to the Roman Destruction of the Temple
sources.author: Hershel Shanks
